title: Hutlar

'''Hutlar''' was a small, [[Temperature/Legends|chilly]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]]. [[Orbit/Legends|Orbiting]] a [[Red dwarf/Legends|red dwarf]] [[Star/Legends|star]], it was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Hutlarian]]s. They served the [[Noble/Legends|aristocratic]] [[Ancient Houses]] of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region in various roles until [[553 BBY]], when their home was liberated from the nobles' control.

==Description==
Hutlar was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Hutlar system/Legends|Hutlar system]] of the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], a part of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The [[Senex-Juvex Loop]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] linked it to the [[Karfeddion system/Legends|Karfeddion]] and [[Nepoy system/Legends|Nepoy systems]].<ref name="TEA" /> Hutlar was a small,<ref name="Hunt" /> dim, and [[Temperature/Legends|chilly]] world that occupied a close [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around a [[Red dwarf/Legends|red dwarf]] [[Star/Legends|star]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
When the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Osted Picutorion]] initiated the [[Crimson Days]], a period of unrest in the Senex-Juvex in [[553 BBY]], one of the terms of the peace settlement was that the [[Hutlarian]]s, the [[Homeworld/Legends|natives]] of Hutlar, be liberated from their servitude to the region's [[Lord/Legends|lords]]. As a result, the Hutlar [[Star system/Legends|system]] became the only one in the region not to be under the control of the [[Noble/Legends|aristocratic]] [[Ancient Houses]]. Their newfound freedom meant little for the Hutlarians themselves, however, and they continued to serve the nobles in various functions.<ref name="TEA" />

==Inhabitants==
Hutlar was home to the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] Hutlarians. Hutlar's natives served the Ancient Houses of the Senex-Juvex as workers, [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]],<ref name="TEA" /> and [[Shock trooper/Legends|shock troops]].<ref name="Hunt" />

==Behind the scenes==
Hutlar was first mentioned in "[[The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 2]]," a [[Wizards.com]] [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] article containing "Mission 2: Destroying the Past," a scenario for use with the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Miniatures|''Star Wars'' Miniatures]] game. The article was authored by [[Jason Fry]] and published on [[December 9]], [[2004]].<ref name="Hunt" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the world in grid square L-17.<ref name="TEA" />
